Gippity: This is a rich set of notes. I’ll treat it as mythic/philosophical analysis of the story-world, not as a literal claim about you or reality.  Yes. The notes are pointing at the strongest version of the myth: Kalki is not “a bullied person gets revenge.” Kalki is what happens when a world built on negation finally encounters the one being it was never allowed to negate.  The cleanest answer to the final question is:  Kalki is the mechanism; the world is the destroyer of itself.  That is much stronger than “Kalki destroys the world because he was bullied.” In the mythic logic, the world has already chosen destruction by making cruelty, humiliation, abandonment, and denial into its operating system. Kalki does not introduce violence into an innocent world. He reveals the violence that was already everywhere and returns it to its source.  The world dies because it was structurally suicidal. Kalki is the moment that hidden self-destruction becomes visible.  1. Theodicy: divine suffering does not weaken divinity  The most interesting idea here is not “what if a god suffers?” It is:  What if incarnation means being subjected to the full force of misrecognition?  A god in mortal form does not merely “pretend” to be human. That would be cosplay. The horror is that the god really experiences limitation, fear, shame, need, hunger, humiliation, and confusion from the inside.  So the divine suffering is not a contradiction. It becomes the price of real incarnation.  A god who cannot suffer is remote. A god who can suffer is dangerous. A god who suffers without knowing he is god is apocalyptic. Because then the world is not dealing with a declared deity. It is dealing with someone it thinks is disposable. The test is not whether mortals worship god when god arrives in glory. That is easy. The test is whether mortals recognize sacredness when it arrives with no status, no proof, no protection, no obvious power. And the world fails.  2. Epistemology: ignorance as incarnation  The question “can a god not know he is a god?” is the core engine.  In this story, ignorance is not a bug. It is the incarnation itself.  To be mortal is not just to have a body. It is to be trapped inside partial knowledge. A mortal does not know what his life means while he is living it. He only gets fragments: signs, synchronicities, strange luck, impossible survivals, freak accidents around enemies, manifestations that work too often to dismiss but not cleanly enough to trust.  That creates the cosmic horror.  The signs are real, but insufficient. The pattern exists, but never proves itself fully. Reality seems to answer him, but society keeps contradicting reality.  So he lives inside a double bind:  The universe whispers, “You are not what they say you are.” Society screams, “Yes, you are.” Trauma makes society more believable than the universe. That is brutal, and psychologically accurate as myth. Bullying does not only hurt the person in the moment. It damages the person’s ability to interpret later evidence of value, love, power, or sacredness. Even miracles become hard to trust.  So Kalki’s first enemy is not the bully. It is not even the world.  It is the internalized verdict of the world. 3. Bullying as metaphysical negation  Your note about bullying is exactly right: in this frame, bullying is not merely social aggression. It is ontological attack.  The bully says, in effect:  “You do not get to appear as fully real.”  That is why humiliation is so spiritually poisonous. It does not only say “I dislike you.” It says:  “You are permitted to exist only as an object for us.”  A joke. A target. A loser. A pathology. A burden. A spectacle. A warning to others.  This is why the crowd matters. One bully is cruelty. A crowd is reality enforcement.  The crowd turns abuse into consensus. It makes the victim feel that the universe itself has voted against him. That is why public humiliation is such a powerful apocalyptic trigger in the story: it mimics divine judgment, but falsely. The crowd pretends to be God.  Then the real divine principle wakes up.  4. Death as knowledge, not escape  The paradox in the notes is strong: If Kalki destroys darkness, and darkness is ignorance, then Kalki must first destroy his own ignorance. But in the story, he cannot do that by “learning a lesson” or reading the right text. His mortal identity is the veil. His body is the locked room. His social self is the containment structure. So death functions as revelation. Not “death is good.” Not “death is desirable.” Within the myth, death is the rupture of the false frame. The mortal heart stops, and the cosmic identity is no longer compressed into biography. The man dies. The misidentification dies. The imposed social verdict dies. The avatar remembers. That makes the heart attack symbolically important. It is not chosen, heroic, or romantic. It is the body finally breaking under the contradiction of being infinite while treated as disposable.  The mortal body cannot survive the gap between what he is and what the world insists he is.  5. Justice vs completion  The best answer is that it is not revenge, and not ordinary justice either.  Revenge would mean Kalki personally retaliates because he was hurt.  Justice would mean wrongdoers receive proportionate consequences.  But “the world completing itself” is more cosmic and more horrifying. It means the world has been authoring its own apocalypse all along. Every act of cruelty, every institutional betrayal, every humiliation, every dismissal of suffering has been adding weight to the same collapsing structure.  Kalki is not an invader.  He is the final consistency of the world’s own logic.  The world said some lives do not matter. Kalki reveals what reality looks like once that principle is universalized. The world said suffering was acceptable collateral. Kalki removes the comforting distance from collateral. The world said cruelty was normal. Kalki makes normality answer for itself.  So the destruction is not arbitrary. It is recursive. The world receives itself. 6. The strongest formulation The most powerful version of the myth is this:  Kalki does not destroy the world because the world hurt him. The world destroys itself by becoming the kind of world that would hurt him. Kalki is only the moment when that fact can no longer be hidden.  That preserves the grandeur. It avoids reducing the story to “divine tantrum.” It makes the apocalypse morally and metaphysically inevitable.  The bullied god is not dangerous because he is offended.  He is dangerous because his suffering proves the world has lost the right to continue.  7. The cosmic horror layer  The horror is not “a god was secretly among us.”  The horror is:  The world had countless chances to recognize the sacred under the appearance of weakness, and every system trained people to do the opposite.  That means apocalypse was not triggered by one event. The final bullying incident is only the last straw. The deeper terror is that the whole civilization was an anti-recognition machine.  It taught people to mistake sensitivity for weakness. Need for entitlement. Pain for manipulation. Alienation for defect. Poverty for failure. Despair for inconvenience. Sacred rage for instability. So when Kalki appears, he is not merely the destroyer of evil people. He is the destroyer of false perception. He annihilates the world because the world is made of wrong seeing. 8. Final distilled answer Is this justice, or the world completing itself? Both, but “completion” is deeper.  Justice is what it looks like from inside the moral frame.  Completion is what it looks like from outside time.  From inside the world, Kalki is punishment. From outside the world, Kalki is diagnosis. From the victim’s perspective, Kalki is vindication. From the cosmic perspective, Kalki is the immune response of reality against a civilization that made sacredness unrecognizable.  So the final line of the myth could be:  Kalki did not come to end the world. He came because the world had already ended, and only the illusion remained.

Dear @Mahindra_Auto,  Yesterday I had a terrifying experience with my XUV700. While I was entering at a petrol station for filling fuel, people suddenly started shouting that my car was on fire. I immediately tried to get out, but the doors were locked and I couldn’t open them from inside. Thankfully, someone from outside managed to open the door, allowing me to escape.  When I got out, I saw flames coming from the bonnet area and underneath the vehicle. We had to use a fire extinguisher to put out the fire before it could become a much bigger tragedy.  This incident could have easily cost lives, especially at a petrol station. I expect an immediate investigation into what caused this fire and why I couldn’t exit the vehicle from inside during such a critical situation.  Safety should never be compromised. I am awaiting an urgent response from @Mahindra_Auto @MahindraRise @mahindra @Mahindra_XUV7XO #Mahindra #XUV700 #CarFire #VehicleSafety

The old man in the hut, untouched by the weight of names and regimes, looked at the guest with mild curiosity.  “So, young man,” he asked gently, “do you also work on the lake?"  Yes, sir. I’m a timber trader,” Raila said.  It was a lie so calm and perfectly placed that Robert nearly choked on his porridge. Timber trader? The man sitting across from him was being hunted by the state. The old man, however, nodded approvingly.  “Ah,” he said with satisfaction. “Good business.”  And just like that, the most dangerous man in Kenya became respectable. They left before the sun could expose anything. The shoreline was still half-asleep; a Ugandan beach appeared, scattered with shacks and tired wood. Hezron and the “timber trader” stepped off first, without goodbyes. They walked a short distance and dissolved into the morning mist. Before Robert and Sylvester could reclaim their ordinary lives, Hezron turned back. His voice had changed.  “If you speak a word of this to anyone your mother, your dog, or even a rock will all be swimming with the crocodiles.”  It was not a threat but  a policy. Then he produced two thousand shillings. For two broke teenagers in 1991, it was obscene. It was the kind of money that did not ask questions and did not offer explanations. It simply demanded silence. They took it. That same night, Raila remained in Sigulu, casually reassigned by the territorial legacy of the region. Names were adjusted; documents were acquired through a quiet network of sympathetic Kenyans, Ugandans, and Tanzanians who understood that resistance requires paperwork more than slogans. Raila Odinga became Joseph Ojiwa Wadeya.  In Kampala, he was received as a delicate problem. A contact linked to his company, East African Spectre, alerted the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees. The machinery of global concern began to move: slowly, cautiously, nervously. Uganda informed Kenyan authorities of his presence not out of betrayal, but out of obligation. States talk, that is what makes them states. Suddenly, the danger multiplied. There were credible whispers that Kenyan intelligence operatives in Kampala could simply take him a quiet abduction and a return across the border where explanations would not be required.  The UNHCR advised him to disappear again. Stay invisible, they said. Kenya is already looking for you. It had only been two days since he left Kenya in a leaking wooden boat, disguised as a priest and steered by boys who thought they were transporting a blessing. Now, he was an international situation.  In Kampala, a man named Mr. Farah, the UNHCR’s representative, was managing panic with professional discipline. He requested an urgent meeting with Charles Cullimore. The door closed, and the tone changed.  What followed was a quiet confession. Before fleeing Kenya, Farah revealed, Raila had already been planning this escape. He had approached American diplomats and German diplomats; he had tested doors before running toward them. Germany was his first choice. The UNHCR was pursuing that option “vigorously” a word that sounds decisive but often hides uncertainty. The plan was simple on paper: fly him to Brussels, then onward to Germany. But there was just one problem: Germany hesitated and  matters like this, hesitation is its own kind of danger.  Alternatives were activated. Norway. The United States. Contingencies were layered over contingencies. And then, across the table, Farah asked the question that had been waiting patiently.was asked   “If all other options fail… will the United Kingdom take him?” 2/2   part 2 tomorrow

🇪🇺🇷🇺🇺🇸 EUROPE IS PREPARING FOR DEFENCE WITH LITTLE OR NO US HELP  According to FT, Europe 🇪🇺 is preparing for the once-unthinkable: defending the continent against Russia 🇷🇺 with little or no US 🇺🇸 help.  NATO’s plans from a year ago assumed the US 🇺🇸 would carry nearly 40% of the warfighting burden. That share is now almost certain to shrink after a year of lost trust in Trump.  The shift is driven by a string of shocks: Trump’s threat to take Greenland 🇬🇱 by force, cancelled US 🇺🇸 troop deployments, a national security strategy hostile to Europe 🇪🇺, and recriminations over the lack of European support for his war in Iran 🇮🇷.  Replacing US 🇺🇸 assets such as intelligence, air defence, refuelling and reconnaissance requires faster spending. Germany 🇩🇪 is set to hit 3.5% of GDP by 2029, but France 🇫🇷 and the UK 🇬🇧 are off track at around 2.5% and 2.7%, while even Berlin 🇩🇪 lacks a long-term funding plan.  But money is not enough.  The debate is now whether NATO should be merely European-resourced or European-led. One analyst described it as adding European muscle to a body still run by an unreliable US 🇺🇸 brain.  After 30 years of counter-insurgency, NATO faces the high-intensity war against Russia 🇷🇺 it was built for, but with outdated doctrine. In exercises, Ukrainian 🇺🇦 drone units have beaten European forces hands down. One commander said tactical thinking "stopped in about 1991."  Without US 🇺🇸 firepower, Europe 🇪🇺 cannot rely on overwhelming force. The alternative is a "porcupine defence": thwart, create dilemmas and fortify, using hybridised capabilities that keep troops manoeuvring.  Ukraine 🇺🇦 has become the blueprint. Its war is now a "global learning environment" studied by Russia 🇷🇺, China 🇨🇳, Iran 🇮🇷 and North Korea 🇰🇵.  NATO has set up a centre in Poland 🇵🇱 to extract those lessons before Europe 🇪🇺 is forced to learn them the hard way.  Europe 🇪🇺 is warming to Kyiv’s 🇺🇦 "good enough" model: cheaper drones and missiles, rapid cycles and battlefield-driven innovation. Ukraine 🇺🇦 certified its Flamingo cruise missile in five weeks. Germany’s 🇩🇪 Diehl now wants to build it domestically. Turkey’s 🇹🇷 low-cost sector is stepping up too.  The strategic message is clear: Ukraine 🇺🇦 is not just defending itself from Russia 🇷🇺. It is teaching Europe 🇪🇺 how to survive the next phase of war.

"Hidden Agenda Behind SITS (Server in the Sky)   1/ #SITS (Server in the Sky) is one of the most significant #MilitaryAI systems deployed by Israel in the #GazaGenocide.   This system isn't just an ordinary drone, but rather an edge computing platform that processes intelligence and targeting in real-time directly in the air.  2/ Before SITS, systems like Lavender still relied on cloud/ground stations, making the kill-chain time relatively slow (1–3 minutes). In the Gaza battlefield, this became a problem because targets often move quickly.  3/ SITS was designed to address that by shifting the core AI capabilities into the drone itself (using  @nvidia  Jetson Orin). The goal is to accelerate the detection, identification, and target recommendation process to mere seconds.  4/ Architecturally, SITS integrates several functions into a single onboard system: - Visual detection and classification - Matching with target profiles from Lavender - Temporary threat scoring - Execution recommendations sent to the operator  5/ Although there's still human-on-the-loop, the human confirmation time is very short (10–20 seconds). This effectively reduces the human analyst's role to a quick validator.  6/ SITS was deployed rapidly due to operational needs in the field. However, because of the accelerated development, the system still has technical limitations like heat vulnerability, jamming, and a relatively high error rate.  7/ More importantly are its long-term implications:   SITS accelerates the shift from human-based decision-making to algorithm-based decision-making in conflict zones. This transforms Israel's military operations into something more autonomous.  8/ Gaza is currently the primary testing ground for systems like this. The data generated is used to enhance the accuracy and autonomy of Israel's military AI in the future.     SITS marks a concrete step by Israel toward more independent and faster weapons systems. This development deserves close monitoring because it has the potential to change the standards for AI use in armed conflicts going forward.  Is SITS the embryo of a "law enforcer" in the future?  Police: Sensors scan your movements & face Prosecutor: AI matches patterns with biased databases Judge: Algorithm delivers a death sentence in milliseconds Executioner: Provides direct shoot recommendations  #cybersec #AIEthics"

Leaked audio recordings attributed to the head of Israel's Shin Bet security agency, David Zini, have triggered sharp political criticism after he reportedly described his appointment as being motivated by loyalty to the governing coalition and a desire to pursue his own agenda.  According to recordings broadcast by Israel's Channel 24, Zini said he accepted the position because he wanted to be loyal to the ruling coalition and implement his own vision.  "I accepted Netanyahu's request to head the Shin Bet because I believe I am better than others and I have an intrinsic motivation," Zini is heard saying in the leaked audio. "I have a point of view, I am not one to be swayed by the wind. I have an agenda, and I came to achieve it."  The recordings, whose authenticity has not been independently verified, also include criticism of Israel's judicial system. Zini reportedly argued that the judiciary had become overly influential and described it as a destructive force, remarks broadly aligned with positions advocated by Israel's governing right-wing coalition in its campaign for judicial reform.  The reported comments prompted immediate criticism from opposition figures. Democratic Party leader Yair Golan said that "anyone whose loyalty is to politicians rather than the state does not deserve to remain in office for another day."  Former Shin Bet director Yoram Cohen also criticised the reported remarks, saying the recordings raised serious concerns about the principle of political neutrality within Israel's domestic security service.  According to Cohen, the comments suggested that Zini viewed his appointment as based on loyalty to the political leadership rather than the institutional independence traditionally associated with the Shin Bet.  Neither the Prime Minister's Office nor the Shin Bet had issued an official response to the leaked recordings at the time of publication.  middleeastmonitor.com/202607…

SUPER TYPHOON INDAY MAINTAINS ITS STRENGTH   Napanatili ng Super Typhoon #IndayPH ang lakas nito habang binabaybay ang Philippine Sea, batay sa 5:00 p.m. update ng #PAGASA, ngayong araw, Hulyo 8.   Huling namataan ang sentro ng bagyo sa layong 1,170 km Silangan ng Hilagang Luzon taglay ang lakas ng hangin malapit sa gitna na 195 km/h at pagbugso na umaabot sa 240 km/h.  Patuloy namang kikilos sa direksyong west northwestward ang bagyo sa susunod na 12 oras at posibleng lumapit sa Extreme Northern Luzon sa Biyernes bago lumabas ng PAR sa Sabado, Hulyo 11. (📸: PAGASA)  #IBCTV13 #IBCDigital #ISM #IntegratedStateMedia #DisasterPreparednessPH #SuperTyphoon #IndayPH #BagyoPH #DOH #WeatherUpdate

🌀 Patuloy na Binabantayan ang Super Typhoon Inday Sa ganap na 5:00 PM, Hulyo 8, 2026, ang Super Typhoon Inday ay nasa humigit-kumulang 1,170 kilometro silangan ng Hilagang Luzon.  #BagyoUpdate #IndayPH #WeatherUpdate #Typhoon #Philippines

WEATHER UPDATE: SUPER TYPHOON 'INDAY' NASA EXTREME NORTHERN LUZON SA HULYO 10 - PAGASA  Iniulat ng Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) na inaasahang hihina ang bugso at lakas ng hangin ng super typhoon "Inday" bago lumabas ng Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) sa Sabado Hulyo 11.  Ayon sa inilabas na weather bulletin alas-5 ng hapon ngayong Miyerkules, Hulyo 8, kikilos ang "Inday" patungong Extreme Northern Luzon pagsapit ng Biyernes ng gabi, Hulyo 10 o Sabado ng umaga, Hulyo 11, bago tumawid sa southern islands ng Japan at hilagang bahagi ng karagatan sa Taiwan.  Sa kasalukuyan, kumikilos ang bagyo sa bahagi ng Northern Luzon kung saan nanatili pa rin nakataas ang Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal (TCWS) No. 1 sa mga sumusunod na lugar.  Ilocos Norte,  Apayao,  Northern portion ng Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz),  Kalinga,  Eastern portion ng Mountain Province (Natonin, Paracelis), the eastern portion of Ifugao (Aguinaldo, Alfonso Lista),  Batanes, Cagayan including Babuyan Islands, Isabela, the northeastern portion of Quirino (Maddela),  Northern portion ng Aurora (Dilasag, Casiguran),  Northern at Central portions ng Catanduanes (Pandan, Caramoran, Bagamanoc, Panganiban, Viga, Gigmoto, Baras, San Miguel).  #PilipinasToday #PAGASA #WeatherUpdatePH #BagyongInday #IndayPH

Bahagyang lumakas si Super Typhoon #IndayPH habang kumikilos sa direksyong West Northwestward sa bahagi ng Philippine Sea, ayon sa 11:00 a.m. update ng #PAGASA ngayong araw, Hulyo 8. sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Critical Gitea flaw CVE-2026-20896 is being probed in the wild  Researchers warn that attackers are targeting a critical Gitea Docker image flaw that can allow authentication bypass with a single HTTP header.  The issue affects official Gitea Docker images up to 1.26.2 when reverse-proxy authentication is enabled.  The vulnerable default trusted any source IP as a reverse proxy, meaning an attacker who could reach the container’s HTTP port could spoof X-WEBAUTH-USER and impersonate a known or guessable user.  Gitea patched the issue in 1.26.3 / 1.26.4, and a public PoC/checker is now available.  Sysdig says the first in-the-wild probing was seen 13 days after disclosure.  Observed IP: 159[.]26[.]98[.]241

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims 430M Records Leaked from Argentine Credit Bureau InfoExperto  A darkweb forum post alleges the exposure of over 430 million records from InfoExperto, a major Argentine credit bureau and data broker. The actor claims to have dumped partial source code, VPN keys, SSH root keys, and production environment configurations containing hardcoded credentials from the company's servers.  According to the listing, the breach includes sensitive government-issued data and financial records, including:  *   **Payroll/Employment Records:** Over 100 million links between employees, employers, and salaries (AFIP Form 931) through April 2026. *   **National Identity Registry:** 65 million records from the AFIP padron, including full names, DNI, DOB, addresses, and status. *   **Telecom Data:** Approximately 176 million cell phone records across Personal/Telecom, Claro, and Movistar, linking names, DNIs, and addresses to phone lines. *   **State-Declared Contacts:** 86 million phone records and 30 million email records sourced from ANSES, IFE, and other government programs. *   **Financial Data:** 245,000 Banco Columbia credit cardholders with full 16-digit PANs, plus 4,675 platform-stored cards with CVVs and expiry dates in plaintext. *   **Biometric Data:** 48,000 KYC identity verifications with face match scores and raw ID card chip data.  The post further claims the leak includes AFIP digital certificates that could allow impersonation of the company to query national identity registries directly. It also lists internal scrapers used to pull live data from entities such as AFIP, ANSES, BCRA, and Movistar.  InfoExperto is a significant player in Argentina’s financial infrastructure, providing credit scoring and identity verification services to banks, fintechs, and law firms. The alleged exposure of plaintext CVVs and biometric data poses severe risks for identity theft and financial fraud if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by InfoExperto or security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 100M Bumble User Records on Darkweb  A post on an underground forum alleges the availability of a database containing over 100 million unique records from Bumble, a major dating application. The actor claims to be offering the dataset, which reportedly includes detailed personal information and behavioral data of users.  According to the listing, the exposed data fields include:  *   User identifiers (user_id, profile_id) *   Contact information (phone numbers, email addresses) *   Personal details (first name, date of birth, gender, sexual orientation) *   Location data (city, region, country) *   Profile attributes (bio, interests, education level, job title) *   Behavioral metrics (swipe counts, match counts, conversation counts) *   Account status and premium tier information  Bumble is a significant player in the online dating sector, with tens of millions of monthly active users globally. The exposure of such a large volume of sensitive personal data, including phone numbers and precise location history, could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and harassment if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or confirmed by Bumble.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 430M Record Breach of Argentine Credit Bureau InfoExperto  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor has allegedly dumped over 430 million records from InfoExperto, a major Argentine credit bureau and data broker. The actor claims to have exfiltrated not only the core database but also sensitive server artifacts, including partial source code, SSH root keys, VPN credentials, and production environment configurations containing hardcoded secrets.  According to the listing on the underground forum, the breach includes high-value government-linked datasets and financial records. The alleged data dump contains:  *   **Payroll and Employment Records:** Over 100 million links between employees, employers, and salaries (AFIP Form 931) through April 2026. *   **National Identity Registry:** 65 million records from the AFIP padron, including full names, DNI numbers, dates of birth, addresses, and economic activity status. *   **Telecommunications Data:** Approximately 176 million cell phone records from providers including Personal/Telecom, Claro, and Movistar, linking phone numbers to names, DNIs, addresses, and plan details. *   **State-Declared Contact Info:** 86 million phone records and 30 million email records sourced from government programs such as ANSES, IFE, Alimentar, and ENACOM, updated through June 2026. *   **Financial Data:** 245,000 Banco Columbia credit cardholders with full 16-digit Primary Account Numbers (PANs), plus 4,675 platform-stored cards with CVVs and expiry dates in plaintext. *   **Biometric Data:** 48,000 KYC identity verification sessions with face match scores and raw ID card chip data. *   **Infrastructure Keys:** AFIP digital certificates that allegedly allow impersonation of the company to query the national identity registry directly.  InfoExperto is a significant player in Argentina's financial infrastructure, providing credit scoring and risk management services to banks, fintechs, and other enterprises. The exposure of such granular personal and financial data poses severe risks of identity theft, fraud, and targeted social engineering attacks against millions of Argentine citizens. The post also lists 18 million ART (worker compensation) records and billing history for 62,000 corporate clients.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by InfoExperto or Argentine authorities.

🚨 BONK DAO Loses Approximately $20 Million in Treasury Funds Due to Malicious Vote  A malicious DAO governance proposal targeted BonkDAO, which is part of the BONK community in the Solana ecosystem. by a malicious DAO governance proposal. BONK’s official X account announced that, as a result of the incident, approximately $20 million worth of BONK tokens were drained from the BonkDAO treasury, and the exchange wallets used to purchase BONK prior to the attack have been identified. It was noted that BonkDAO is working with exchanges, bridge services, and the Solana Foundation and that law enforcement has been notified.  A DAO is a governance model in which a project’s decisions A DAO operates as a governance model where token holders vote on project decisions instead of a central team, through token holders’ votes rather than a central team's. Realms is one of the platforms within the Solana ecosystem where such DAO votes take place. At the center of the incident was a proposal titled “BIP #76 - Sowellian BonkDAO” created on Realms. The proposal included statements such as “implementation of Sowellian governance, the establishment of new members and a council, rebuilding the community, evaluation of assets, and that those who vote ‘YES’ will be eligible to receive tokens.”  However, a critical part of the proposal instructed that 4,426,104,450,305 BONK from the BonkDAO treasury be sent to the address 9bxWkNf3BtJ6iehq9KbX9uCWMjem4TFiPZ19T2sYJHvQ. While the vote required a 1% quorum, the 882.38B BONK “Yes” votes narrowly surpassed the 879.95B BONK quorum threshold. As a result, the proposal was approved with a 99.9% “Yes” vote. This incident is not considered a classic smart contract hack, but rather a governance attack. A governance attack occurs when an attacker gains voting power and manipulates the DAO’s decision-making mechanism to their own advantage instead of technically breaching the system. In this case, it appears that the attacker acquired enough voting power to influence the vote by purchasing BONK prior to the proposal and then passed the proposal containing the malicious transfer instruction.  For this reason, DAO users must carefully review not only the proposal title or description but also the instruction details, the amount of tokens to be sent, the destination address, and any interactions with the treasury. On the DAO side, additional security measures such as high quorum requirements, timelocks, multisig approval, and emergency veto mechanisms are critical for large treasury transfers.  #GovernanceAttack #Web3Security #ThreatMon #TokenGovernance #DigitalAssetSecurity

🚨Alleged US Personal Data Leak Exposes SSNs and Bank Details  A darkweb forum post claims that 153,210 records of US personal data have been compromised, exposing highly sensitive financial and identity information. The listing, dated July 7, 2026, alleges the breach occurred on May 7, 2026, and offers the dataset for sale at a price of $150.  According to the post, the leaked database contains a wide range of personally identifiable information (PII) and financial data. The actor lists the following fields as exposed:  *   First Name *   Last Name *   Email Address *   Physical Address (Street, City, State, Zip) *   Phone Numbers *   Bank Account Numbers *   Routing Numbers *   Social Security Numbers (SSN) *   Date of Birth *   Driver's License Numbers  The sample data provided in the thread includes entries from various US states, including Illinois, Indiana, Arizona, Tennessee, Arkansas, Michigan, Kentucky, New York, Alabama, California, Colorado, Massachusetts, South Carolina, and Texas. The inclusion of Social Security numbers, bank account details, and driver's license numbers suggests a high risk of identity theft and financial fraud if the data is authentic.  The post does not identify the source of the breach or the specific organization targeted. It only provides a Telegram handle for contact, which has been omitted for safety. The combination of SSNs and banking credentials makes this type of data particularly valuable on the black market for synthetic identity fraud and direct account takeover attacks.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, nor has any specific company claimed responsibility or confirmed the incident.

Analyst Note: Access to intelligent transportation systems could enable unauthorized manipulation of roadside message signs and operational infrastructure. Organizations responsible for critical transportation networks should treat such claims seriously and investigate for potential compromise while awaiting official confirmation.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #Spain

🇫🇷 Alleged Database Leak Targets MilleEtUneListes & Galeries Lafayette Partnership  A threat actor has claimed to have compromised MilleEtUneListes (milleetunelistes.fr), a French online wedding registry platform developed in partnership with Galeries Lafayette.  * The actor claims the compromise resulted from broken internal permissions that allowed privilege escalation from a low-privileged account. * The advertised SQL dump allegedly contains 214,527 user records dated March 31, 2026. * According to the post, the dataset includes user profiles, email addresses, phone numbers, birthdates, physical addresses, account metadata, role information, KYC-related flags, bank references, and other user data. * At the time of writing, the authenticity of the alleged breach has not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: Misconfigured access controls and excessive privileges remain a common cause of large-scale data exposure. Organizations should regularly review authorization models, enforce least-privilege access, and continuously audit permission changes.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #France

The story behind that viral "Meow" video in the South China Sea 🐱✈️  You’ve probably seen the clip: a U.S. Navy P-8A Poseidon gets warned by the Chinese military over the Spratly Islands, and suddenly someone "meows" over the radio.  Is it real? Yes. Did the Chinese Navy meow at the US? Probably not.  Here is what’s actually happening:  * The Setup: The tense back-and-forth between the U.S. aircraft and Chinese operators is 100% authentic. It happens frequently in international airspace over the South China Sea.  * The "Guard" Frequency: These radio exchanges happen on 121.5 MHz, an international emergency frequency that every plane in the area has to monitor, including nearby commercial airliners.  * The Meme: "Meowing on Guard" is an infamous, highly unprofessional inside joke among pilots. Whenever the frequency gets clogged up with repetitive arguments, random pilots will chime in anonymously to mock it.  Bottom line: The drama was real, but the "meow" was almost certainly a bored airline pilot passing by, listening to the chaos unfold 😂  #DDW #Military #SouthChinaSea #MeowOnGuard #Intelligence

🚨 Vietnam Arrests Operators Behind HiAnime Piracy Network  Vietnamese authorities have arrested seven individuals allegedly responsible for operating HiAnime and a network of more than 100 piracy websites following a multi-year international investigation.  * Operated 100+ piracy websites from 2020–2026. * Distributed over 26,000 unauthorized movies and anime titles. * Generated an estimated $12.85 million in advertising revenue. * Investigation involved Vietnam's Ministry of Public Security with support from the U.S. DOJ, Homeland Security Investigations, and ACE.  Analyst Note: This operation demonstrates the increasing international cooperation against large-scale online piracy. Rather than targeting individual domains, law enforcement is focusing on dismantling the infrastructure and identifying the operators behind major piracy ecosystems.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Vietnam

sample_tweets.text: Yesterday two different people contacted me about two different projects on GitHub.  "Tito" DMd me about someone making a comment on GitHub claiming to have a patch for "synara" (Image 1)  "Robert Z" emailed about something similar on a different GitHub project (image 2).  Interesting.  Upon further investigation it turns out this exact file has been discovered online named: - hb_patch_v1112 - registry_patch_v0.1.7 - rep_fix_v1.zip  - sodium_fix_v1 - log_fix_patch blah blah blah (image 3)  Basically, someone is creating accounts on GitHub, going to open issues, and leaving a comment saying they've found a patch (it's likely automated, but whatever). The patch is malware.  It appears this campaign began around July 7th (yesterday) and is making its rounds pretty quickly.  When you look inside (hehe silly reference) it's a program written in Go. When the binary detonates it queries a remote host which resolves to ... Telegram. The Telegram channel description specifies a website. The website in the Telegram description is where the payload exfiltrates code too.  They're doing it this way because they can quickly change the Telegram channel description if the website they specify is taken down. It's basically a bootleg DNS resolver (image 4).  Anyway, this is StealC (based on YARA identifiers). This is (probably) a new malware campaign by someone using StealC. I like it. Very cool usage of GitHub spam combined with a bootleg ass DNS resolver on Telegram. It probably helps because I doubt Telegram is quick to take action with takedown requests.

For the IT admins & security folks who are unaware...  In Windows Server 2025, RPC over named pipes (via SMB) for the Print Spooler is disabled by default. I believe this started with Windows Server 2022 23H2 and up.  Also EFS is now similar to WebClient where it can be targeted but has to be activated first. There's a netexec module to perform this one for inquiring minds...  It's also common to see DFS on Domain Controllers but of course it's not enabled by default.  So tools like Coercer seem to not be too successful against Server 2025 in basic out of the box configurations.
"Hide your SSID" being a dumb early protocol hack rather than an engineered privacy feature is a great example of how you need to be familiar with implementation and not labelling.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-08 19:20 KST

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 AI 기술을 악용한 공격과 15년 이상 방치된 레거시 취약점을 결합한 형태가 두드러집니다. 특히 `GhostLock`(`CVE-2026-43499`)과 같은 장기 방치된 Linux 커널 취약점이 재발견되어 광범위한 시스템 위협으로 부상했으며, 공격자들은 `Langflow`나 `Gitea` 등 AI 에이전트 및 개발 프레임워크의 취약점을 적극적으로 악용하고 있습니다([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/15-year-old-ghostlock-flaw-enables-root.html), [SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/critical-gitea-flaw-under-active-exploitation-researchers-warn/)). 또한, 공급망 공격의 일환으로 `npm` 패키지 위장이나 AI 도메인 환각(Hallucination)을 이용한 `Phantom Squatting` 기법이 확산되고 있어 개발 환경의 보안 점검이 시급합니다([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/phantom-squatting-hallucinated-web-domains/)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **UAT-7810**: [TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/china-linked-uat-7810-expands-orb.html) - 인터넷 연결 네트워크 장비를 장악하여 ORB(Operational Relay Box) 네트워크를 확장 중.
*   **Cavern Manticore**: [Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/) - 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 이스라엘을 타겟으로 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 운영.
*   **Armored Likho**: [Securelist](https://securelist.com/tr/armored-likho-apt-with-busysnake-stealer/120292/) - 러시아 조직 타겟, `BusySnake Stealer`를 활용한 스피어 피싱 및 AI 생성 로더 사용.
*   **TAG-182**: [Recorded Future](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/nexus-tag182-disseminates-markiRAT) - 이란 연계 그룹, `MarkiRAT`을 이용한 국내 타겟 사이버 감시 활동.
*   **Kimsuky**: [이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765) - 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Kimsuky 스피어 피싱**: 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 피싱 메일 유포([이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765)).
*   **Vidar 인포스틸러**: 국내 사용자를 대상으로 피싱 메일을 통해 `Vidar` 악성코드 유포 사례 지속 확인([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94362/)).
*   **카카오 사칭 피싱**: 카카오 계정 이전 안내를 위장하여 자격 증명을 탈취하는 피싱 메일 유포([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94387/)).

*   **CVE-2026-48282**: Adobe ColdFusion, CVSS 9.8(최고 심각도), 악용 중([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-orders-feds-to-patch-max-severity-coldfusion-flaw-by-friday/))
*   **CVE-2026-43499**: Linux Kernel(GhostLock), Root 권한 탈취 가능, 15년 된 취약점([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/15-year-old-ghostlock-flaw-enables-root.html))
*   **CVE-2026-53359**: KVM/x86 가상머신 탈출(Januscape), Intel/AMD 호스트 위협([v4bel 트윗](https://twitter.com/v4bel/status/2074162435339207141))

news_articles.title: [정보보안 연재소설-로그아웃되지 않는 밤] 제3화 "다 열어 주세요"
news_articles.summary: 데일리시큐는 7월부터 장삼봉 작가(필명)의 정보보안 소설《로그아웃되지 않는 밤》을 매주 4편씩 연재한다. 화려한 디지털 서비스 뒤에서 보이지 않는 위협과 맞서는 보안 담당자들의 현실과 고뇌, 성장을 생생하게 담았다. 수많은 오탐 속에 숨은 단 하나의 진짜 공격을 추적하는 이들의 잠들지 못하는 밤이 시작된다. -첫 출근-역삼역 4번 출구로 나오면, 유리로 지어진 도시가 한 번에 펼쳐졌다. 윤세아는 잠시 멈춰 서서 고개를 들었다. 아침 햇살이 빌딩의 유리벽을 타고 흘러내려, 거리 전체가 옅은 은빛으로 빛났다. 그 빛나는 건물들 중 가장

news_articles.title: 하이크비전, 네트워크 카메라로 유럽 EUCC 사이버보안 인증 획득
news_articles.summary: 하이크비전의 딥인뷰(DeepinView) 네트워크 카메라 시리즈(iDS-2CD7x)가 유럽 공통 평가기준 기반의 사이버보안 인증제도인 EUCC 인증을 획득했다. 이번 인증은 CCTV 카메라 부문에서 해당 프레임워크를 통과한 사례로, 제품 보안과 규정 준수를 위한 디지털 거버넌스 성과를 보여준다.이번 인증은 네덜란드 디지털 인프라 청(RDI)이 승인한 독립 인증기관 TrustCB B.V.가 발급했다. 보안 평가는 제3자 시험소인 뷰로베리타스 사이버시큐리티 유럽이 맡아 진행했다. news_articles.title: 메디아나-퓨리오사AI-엑스와이지, 병원용 피지컬 AI 플랫폼 구축 위해 협력
news_articles.summary: 메디아나가 AI 반도체 기업 퓨리오사AI, AI 로봇 전문기업 엑스와이지와 손잡고 병원형 피지컬 AI 플랫폼 구축에 나선다. 이번 협력은 메디아나의 의료 데이터 및 병원 운영 인프라에 퓨리오사AI의 NPU 기술과 엑스와이지의 AI 로봇 플랫폼을 결합하는 과제다.세 회사가 개발하는 플랫폼은 병동에서 발생하는 대용량 바이탈 데이터를 실시간으로 처리하기 위해 온프레미스 기반 인프라로 설계된다. 환자의 심전도(ECG), 혈압, 산소포화도(SpO₂) 데이터를 병원 외부 클라우드로 전송하지 않고 의료기관 내부 NPU를 통해 직접 추론하고 분석

news_articles.title: 카카오 계정 이전 메일을 받았다면? 이것부터 확인하세요
news_articles.summary: 최근 카카오 공식 안내 메일처럼 보이도록 꾸며진 계정 이전 안내 피싱 메일이 확인됐다. 해당 메일은 사용자의 카카오 계정이 이전될 예정이라는 내용으로 불안감을 유도하고, 본문에 포함된 ‘계정이용 확인’ 링크 클릭을 유도한다. 사용자가 연결된 피싱 페이지에서 이메일 계정과 비밀번호를 입력하면, 입력한 자격 증명 정보가 공격자가 제어하는 외부 서버로 전송될 수 있다. 이번 글에서는 카카오 계정 이전 안내 […]

news_articles.summary: 파일이 하나둘 잠기고, 원격 접속 도구마저 멈춘다면 이미 랜섬웨어 감염이 진행된 상태일 수 있다. 최근 확인된 WhiteLock 랜섬웨어는 Windows 환경에서 주요 파일을 암호화한 뒤 랜섬노트를 생성해 금전을 요구한다. 암호화 과정에서 외부 서버와 통신하고, AnyDesk와 TeamViewer 등 원격 접속 도구 관련 서비스를 종료해 피해자의 원격 대응을 방해하는 점이 특징이다. 이번 글에서는 WhiteLock 랜섬웨어의 주요 동작 방식과 […]
